title: Supported Comfort
subtitle: Focusing on ease and assistance
description: At this stage, mobility may feel significantly limited, and discomfort can increase. Prioritizing assisted movement, proper rest positioning, and minimizing strain can help maintain comfort and reduce physical stress.
what_to_expect: Very limited mobility during daily activities
Greater reliance on support and assisted movement
daily_safety_tips: Use support or assistance while moving or changing positions
Avoid unnecessary physical effort and conserve energy
Maintain comfortable and supported rest positions
movement_guidelines: Limit movement to essential and gentle activity only
Avoid any movement that causes discomfort or strain
Move slowly and use support at all times
environmental_awareness: Ensure surroundings are safe, stable, and easy to access
Keep pathways clear to prevent trips or imbalance
Continue avoiding exposure to harmful chemicals or smoke
travel_safety: Take frequent breaks to adjust posture if travel is unavoidable
important_note: Assisted movement and proper rest positioning are essential to reduce strain and maintain comfort.
reassurance: With support and careful pacing, you can remain comfortable and safe.
medical_disclaimer: Consult a qualified healthcare professional before starting or modifying any physical activity.

diabetes.personalized_description: Maintaining consistent meals and minimal movement supports stable blood sugar levels during limited mobility.
movement_guidelines: Engage in minimal gentle movement when possible
Maintain consistent meal timing and hydration
Monitor your body's response to reduced activity
diabetes.safety_focus: Watch for fatigue, dizziness, or changes in energy levels.
diabetes.reassurance: Balanced routines support stability and comfort.

anemia.personalized_description: Severe fatigue requires maximum rest and minimal physical effort.
movement_guidelines: Limit activity strictly to essential tasks
Take frequent and extended rest periods
anemia.safety_focus: Be aware of dizziness, weakness, or breathlessness.
anemia.reassurance: Rest and minimal exertion help maintain comfort.

hypertension.personalized_description: Maintaining a calm, low-stress environment supports stable blood pressure.
movement_guidelines: Avoid stressful or physically demanding situations
Engage in minimal and controlled movement
hypertension.safety_focus: Be mindful of stress levels and physical strain.
hypertension.reassurance: A calm and steady routine supports well-being.

thyroid.personalized_description: Consistent routines and minimal activity support metabolic balance during this phase.
movement_guidelines: Maintain a steady and minimal activity pattern
thyroid.safety_focus: Be aware of unusual fatigue or energy fluctuations.

multi_condition_blend.combined_message: When managing multiple conditions, relying on assistance, minimizing movement, and prioritizing rest becomes critical.
priority_guidelines: Avoid unnecessary physical effort or strain
Maintain minimal and essential movement only
Take frequent rest breaks and stay hydrated
multi_condition_blend.reassurance: Support and careful rest help maintain comfort and safety.
